text: I feel as though I am at the intersection of innovation and human curiosity. In the first scene, I observe people gathered behind glass in focused discussion or collaboration, perhaps solving problems or dreaming up new ideas. The space is bright, organized, and calming, yet also slightly impersonal—like so many modern workplaces or study centers. Transitioning to the second scene, vibrant colors and bold graphics announce 'R+D', and the air feels charged with creative energy and potential. Here, experiments, prototypes, and thoughts are openly displayed as if daring the observer to engage. I feel inspired, keenly aware of humanity’s restless drive to question, understand, and build. I feel both participant and observer in the ongoing project of knowledge.
